optionalfeature.name: Injector Projector
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can transfer the contents of a potion you are holding into a syringe and fire it at a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself. The target gains the effects of the potion as if it had drunk it. An unwilling creature can make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC to avoid being injected.

You can also load and fire a syringe with one of your {@subclassFeature Experimental Elixir|Artificer|EFA|Alchemist|EFA|3||Experimental Elixirs} as part of the {@action Magic|XPHB} action you use to create it.

optionalfeature.name: Elixir Diffuser
entries: When you create an {@subclassFeature Experimental Elixir|Artificer|EFA|Alchemist|EFA|3} you can place a special flask stopper on the vial. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, a creature holding the vial can release the flask's contents as a vapor that fills the air in a 5-foot radius around it. Each creature in that area can inhale the vapor to gain the benefit of the elixir as if it had drunk it. However, the duration of the effect is halved, and the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} restored by the {@variantrule Healing|XPHB} option is halved. The Diffuser disappears after it is used, or the elixir in the vial is expended.
Once you create the Diffuser, you cannot do so again until the next dawn.

optionalfeature.name: Empowered Elixir
entries: When you create an {@subclassFeature Experimental Elixir|Artificer|EFA|Alchemist|EFA|3}, its effects are empowered. You choose two effects from the {@subclassFeature Experimental Elixir|Artificer|EFA|Alchemist|EFA|3} table, rather than rolling, and a creature gains both benefits at once on drinking it. You can select the Healing, Swiftness or Resilience benefits more than once, causing the effect to stack - otherwise you must select different options.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Mastered Weapon
entries: When you hit with your {@subclassFeature Arcane Armor|Artificer|EFA|Armorer|EFA|3||Arcane Armor's} special weapon, you can activate a Weapon Mastery property. The property depends on its form, shown in the table below.

optionalfeature.name: Sentinel Armor
entries: Your {@subclassFeature Arcane Armor|Artificer|EFA|Armorer|EFA|3} gains 4 charges. While you are wearing it, when you see a creature within 30 feet of yourself get hit by an attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend a charge, doff your armor and cause it to fly toward the target. The target's AC increases until it is equal to your AC while you're wearing the armor, potentially turning the hit into a miss. The target retains this AC until the start of your next turn, at which point the armor returns to you and you don it again (no action required).
Alternatively, if you are hit by an attack while you aren't wearing the armor, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend a charge and don it, provided it is within 30 feet of you.
The armor regains {@dice 1d4} charges daily at dawn.

optionalfeature.name: Adaptive Armor
entries: You have fused your {@subclassFeature Arcane Armor|Artificer|EFA|Armorer|EFA|3} with your body, and can have it quickly adapt to your needs. You can switch your current Armor Model to another as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Additionally, when you doff the armor, you can have it recede into your body, re-emerging when you next don it.

optionalfeature.name: Cannon Launcher
entries: When you create an {@subclassFeature Eldritch Cannon|Artificer|EFA|Artillerist|EFA|3}, you can launch the cannon and send it flying to an unoccupied space within 60 feet of you, instead of within 5 feet of you. The space cannot be behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters} or {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}.

optionalfeature.name: Remote Artillery
entries: When you create an {@subclassFeature Eldritch Cannon|Artificer|EFA|Artillerist|EFA|3}, you can create an arcane link between it and yourself. While it exists, you can see and hear as if you were in its space, and when you cast an Artificer spell, you can originate the spell from the Cannon's space.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Empowered Cannon
entries: When you create an {@subclassFeature Eldritch Cannon|Artificer|EFA|Artillerist|EFA|3}, its firepower is enhanced. Whenever you roll for damage or {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} with the cannon, you add {@dice 1d4} to the roll. If any of the dice roll their maximum value, you can roll those dice again, adding the new result to the damage or {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB|Temporary Hit Point} total. This process is repeated if the rerolled dice roll their maximum value again, and so on.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Mounting Support
entries: You make your {@creature Steel Defender|EFA} more suitable to ride as a mount. The {@creature Steel Defender|EFA|Defender's} {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet, and you can use the {@creature Steel Defender|EFA|Defender} as a mount, even if your size is Medium.

optionalfeature.name: Vigilant Deflector
entries: You make your {@creature Steel Defender|EFA|Defender} even more protective of your allies on the battlefield. When a creature within 15 feet of it makes an attack, it can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to 10 feet toward that creature without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} and immediately use Deflect Attack.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Artificial Intelligence
entries: You upgrade the mental capabilities of your {@creature Steel Defender|EFA}. The {@creature Steel Defender|EFA|Defender's} Intelligence and Charisma scores increase to 14, it can speak the languages it knows, and it becomes capable of making complex decisions with only minor direction. You can command your {@creature Steel Defender|EFA|Defender} on your turn without using your {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Maps of Awareness
entries: A creature holding a map of your {@subclassFeature Adventurer's Atlas|Artificer|EFA|Cartographer|EFA|3} is made wary of its surroundings. It adds {@dice 1d4} to Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks and Dexterity saving throws.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Prescribed Fire
entries: When you cast {@spell Faerie Fire|XPHB} without a spell slot using your {@subclassFeature Mapping Magic|Artificer|EFA|Cartographer|EFA|3||Illuminated Cartography} feature, {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on that spell doesn't prevent you from {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on other Artificer spells.

optionalfeature.name: Mapmaker's Repositioning
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cause any number of creatures holding a map of your {@subclassFeature Adventurer's Atlas|Artificer|EFA|Cartographer|EFA|3} to teleport. All targets must be on this plane of existence. You determine the destination for each creature, which must be an unoccupied space you can see within 120 feet of yourself. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Shielding Panel
entries: When you use your {@subclassFeature Force Panel|Artificer|EFA|Forceforger|ArtExpansion|3} feature's Damage Redirection, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to redirect damage from each creature of your choice within 5 feet of the panel that also took the triggering damage, instead of just one. The Panel takes a separate instance of damage for each creature.

optionalfeature.name: Moving Platform
entries: You can use your {@subclassFeature Force Panel|Artificer|EFA|Forceforger|ArtExpansion|3} feature in a special way, requiring your {@status Concentration|XPHB} when you do so, as if you were {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on a spell. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on each of your turns, you can cause the panel to move up to 30 feet through space, carrying with it any creatures or objects it is supporting. The panel's movement fails to move unwilling creatures or move through the space of structures, coming to a halt on attempting to do so.
If you stop {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on this effect, the panel remains in place until it is destroyed.

optionalfeature.name: Personalized Force Shields
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can use your {@subclassFeature Field Projector|Artificer|EFA|Forceforger|ArtExpansion|3} to bolster one willing creature that you touch with a flexible force field that surrounds its body. It gains a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Artificer level.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Improved Flux
entries: While your {@subclassFeature Magnetic Manipulator|Artificer|EFA|Magneticist|ArtExpansion|3} is on your person, all metal Melee weapons gain the {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} property for you, with a normal range of 40 feet and a long range of 80 feet, unless they were already higher.

optionalfeature.name: Magnetodexterity
entries: When you use your {@subclassFeature Magnetic Manipulator|Artificer|EFA|Magneticist|ArtExpansion|3||Move Metal} feature, you can simultaneously create a number of {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB|Mage Hands} equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 2), and can control each of them with the same {@action Magic|XPHB} action.

optionalfeature.name: Magnetic Levitation
entries: While your {@subclassFeature Magnetic Manipulator|Artificer|EFA|Magneticist|ArtExpansion|3} is on your person and you are wearing metal armor, you have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 50 feet and can hover.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Swift Injector
entries: When you use the Free Casting of your {@subclassFeature Mutagenic Concoctions|Artificer|EFA|Mutagenist|ArtExpansion|3} feature, if the spell has a casting time of an action, you can change it to a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} for that casting.

optionalfeature.name: Mutagenic Samples
entries: When you cast one of the spells you have prepared from your {@subclassFeature Mutagenist Spells|Artificer|EFA|Mutagenist|ArtExpansion|3} feature, you can create a number of mutagenic samples instead of producing the effects of the spell. The number of samples you create is equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum one). These samples form as a liquid, each of which must be stored in its own flask. A creature can consume a sample as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, granting the drinker the effects of the spell until the end of its next turn, as if it had cast the spell on itself. The spell uses your Intelligence as the spellcasting ability, and does not require {@status Concentration|XPHB}. Any remaining samples spoil when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Epic Transmogrification
entries: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, select one of the following spells: {@spell Draconic Transformation|FTD}, {@spell Tasha's Otherworldly Guise|TCE} or {@spell Tenser's Transformation|XGE}. Once, before you finish another {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can cast the chosen spell without expending a spell slot, using your {@subclassFeature Mutagenic Concoctions|Artificer|EFA|Mutagenist|ArtExpansion|3||Concoction Casting} feature. The spell counts as an Artificer spell for you.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Momentum Accelerator
entries: The first time on a turn that a creature travels through a portal created by your {@subclassFeature Portals|Artificer|EFA|Portalweaver|ArtExpansion|3} feature, its momentum is boosted, increasing its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 10 feet until the end of that turn.

optionalfeature.name: Range Extender
entries: When you cast a spell which causes a creature to teleport, you can add your {@subclassFeature Portal Device|Artificer|EFA|Portalweaver|ArtExpansion|3||Portal Range} to the distance teleported.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Programmed Positioning
entries: You upgrade your {@subclassFeature Portal Device|Artificer|EFA|Portalweaver|ArtExpansion|3} with the ability to program the positions of your portals in space relative to yourself, without needing to see. When you create or move the portals of your {@subclassFeature Portals|Artificer|EFA|Portalweaver|ArtExpansion|3} feature, you can cause the portals to appear in spaces within range that you specify by stating distance and direction, such as "60 feet, straight forward". If either of the portals appear in a space that is already occupied by an object or creature, it fails to manifest, and the portals are inactive until you move them to a viable location.
This Upgrade requires you to attune to it to gain the benefit, as you would a magic item.

optionalfeature.name: Problem Solver
entries: You have a knack for developing unique tools and objects which can aid in solving problems, magical and mundane alike. When you use your Tinker's Magic feature, instead of creating a piece of equipment as normal, you can craft a problem solving tool, which is a Tiny object that appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of yourself. You describe the tool's appearance, though it is obviously magical in nature.

When you create the tool, describe a specific ability check. You must list what ability score the check uses, what skill it uses (if any) and what the goal of the check is. While a creature has the tool on its person, if it makes a check that fits your description, it can gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check. The tool then disappears. If unused, the tool lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

For example, you could use this feature to create an item that makes it easier to swim through rough waters, giving a creature {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check made to swim. Alternatively, you could create an item that enhances one's ability to search through a library to find a specific piece of lore, giving a creature {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check to find that lore.

optionalfeature.name: Upgraded Weapons
entries: When you create a magic weapon using your Replicate Magic Item feature, you can instead augment the special weapon of your Armor Model. When you do, the weapon gains the magical properties of the magic item, provided the special weapon is of an eligible weapon type for the weapon.

The weapon can only bear one augmentation in this way at a time. If the magic item would be destroyed, the weapon returns to its normal form. If you switch Armor Model, and the new weapon is no longer an eligible form for the magic item, the weapon cannot use the magical properties. Otherwise, the properties transfer between forms.
While the weapon is augmented in this way, you cannot benefit from the Improved Arsenal benefit of the Improved Armorer feature gained at level 9.

optionalfeature.name: Alchemical Grenades
entries: Through your explorations into alchemy you have learned to make explosive concoctions that can be lobbed at groups of enemies.

entries.name: Free Cantrip
entries: You learn one of the following cantrips of your choice: {@spell Acid Splash|XPHB}, {@spell Fire Bolt|XPHB} or {@spell Poison Spray|XPHB}. Your chosen spell doesn't count against the number of cantrips you know.

entries.name: Creating a Grenade
entries: When you start casting a spell using {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} as the spellcasting focus, you can turn that casting into an Alchemical Grenade. The spell must deal Acid, Fire, Necrotic or Poison damage. You can ignore any Verbal components for this casting of the spell.

entries.name: Alchemical Explosion
entries: When you cast a spell in this way and only target one creature with it, each creature within 5 feet of the target, other than the target itself, must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. A creature that fails its saving throw takes damage of the spell's type equal to half your Artificer level (round up). The spell does not benefit from this feature if it already targets an area. The radius increases to 10 feet when you reach level 9 in this class.

entries.name: Note on Alchemical Grenades and Alchemical Savant
entries: The Alchemical Savant feature, granted to Alchemists at level 5, states that you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of a spell (so long as certain conditions are met). When this feature is used in conjunction with the Alchemical Grenades feature, only the target of the spell takes this extra damage, not the creatures within 5 feet of it.
This is because the damage provided by Alchemical Explosion is separate to the damage of the spell, which is what is modified by Alchemical Savant.

spell.name: Artifice Overcharge
entries: You overcharge the magic within an item, causing a large explosion of magical power. Select one magic item that you can see within range. Each creature within a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on the magic item must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 10d6} Force damage and is pushed 10 feet away from the magic item. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.

A creature holding or wearing the item is affected differently: it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the save, and on a failure it drops the item if it is holding it, or has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition if it is wearing it.

Additionally, you can make an ability check with your spellcasting modifier to temporarily drain the item of its magic. The DC is related to its rarity, shown in the following table. If you succeed on the check, it briefly loses its magical properties, becoming a mundane version of itself until the start of your next turn.

spell.name: Automation Barrage
entries: You conjure three tiny spectral automata, each of which fly towards a creature you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against each target, which occur simultaneously. On a hit, a target takes Force damage equal to {@dice 2d4} plus your spellcasting modifier, and the automaton latches onto the target. The target suffers a -1 penalty to its AC and to its attack rolls for each automaton attached to it. The automata disappear at the start of your next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot:
entries: You conjure one additional automaton for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Automate
entries: You touch an object that isn't already affected by this spell and program a single action into it. When a certain trigger occurs, the object magically springs to life, executing the programmed action and then ending the spell.

The executed action must be one that is directly related to how the target object is used, and takes no more than one action to perform. It will typically be the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action for mundane objects or the {@action Magic|XPHB} action for magic items, but other actions may be possible, at the DM's discretion. The object can move, but no further than 5 feet as part of executing the action. The action cannot involve making an attack roll, and if it requires an ability check, roll it using your appropriate ability score.
You specify the trigger when you finish casting the spell. The action executes immediately after the trigger occurs, regardless of whether you want it to.
For example you could program a potion to administer itself to the creature carrying it when that creature drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

You can optionally cause the material component to be consumed by the spell. If you do, the spell's duration becomes 'until dispelled', and it doesn't end when the programmed action is executed. The action can't be triggered again for 6 seconds. The spell ends early if the object is ever more than 10 feet from where it was when you cast the spell.

spell.name: Dynamo Swarm
entries: You deploy a swarm of extremely small flying automatons at a point within range. The swarm takes up a 10-foot diameter {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}, which can overlap with the space of creatures. When you create the swarm and as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your subsequent turns, you can command it to move up to 30 feet and perform one of the following actions:

entries.name: Dismantle
entries: The swarm starts to dismantle everything in its area. Creatures and objects within the swarm's area must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 4d8} Force damage, or half as much on a successful save. Constructs and objects take twice as much damage.

entries.name: Relocate
entries: The swarm latches onto and carries all Medium or smaller creatures and objects within its area to a new location on the ground within 20 feet. The swarm moves with the targets. Unwilling creatures can resist being moved by succeeding on a Strength saving throw.

entries.name: Retroflection
entries: The constructs project light mimicking the surrounding area until the end of your next turn. While a creature is entirely within the swarm's area during this time, it has the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot:
entries: The diameter of the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} increases to 15 feet when cast with a level 5+ slot.

spell.name: Elemental Strikes

components.m: A vial of alchemical liquid infused with the energy of the chosen element

entries: A willing creature you touch surges with elemental power. Select one damage type when you cast the spell, between Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning or Thunder. For the duration, the target's {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} deal damage of your chosen type, and it can roll a {@dice d8} in place of the normal damage of its {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. It also deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage to the target the first time it hits with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} on a turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot:
entries: The extra damage increases at certain spell slot levels. To {@dice 2d6} for level 3-4, and to {@dice 3d6} for level 5+.

spell.name: Fall Loop
entries: You momentarily open two interconnected portals, one directly beneath a creature you can see within range, and the other just above it. If the creature is not flying, it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or fall through these portals in a loop for 30 feet, before the portals vanish and the creature lands back in its original space and takes falling damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot:
entries: The distance the creature falls increases by 20 feet for each slot level above 1.

spell.name: Imbue Sentience

m.text: a sapphire worth 1,000+ GP, which the spell consumes

entries: You spend the casting time programming arcano-technical pathways within the material component, and then touch the target. The target must be a non-sentient object that is Large or smaller, and must not be the remains of a creature. The target gains sentience, along with an Intelligence, Wisdom and Charisma of 10 and the ability to speak one language you know.

Alternatively, when you finish casting the spell you can transfer the soul of a creature within 5 feet of you (or its corpse) into the target object. The creature's soul must be free and willing, and the creature must either be alive or have died no more than 24 hours before completing the spell's casting. The soul is permanently imbued into the object, which instead gains the target creature's Intelligence, Wisdom and Charisma scores, and knows all the languages it knows. If alive, the target creature's body instantly dies, and cannot be revived while the soul is within the object. If the target object is destroyed, the soul is released, and moves onto its appropriate afterlife.
You decide the following elements of the resulting sentient object when you finish casting the spell.

entries.name: Personality
entries: If the sentience isn't derived from a creature's soul, you determine its general personality, its allegiances and what it knows (which cannot be outside the bounds of your own knowledge). From the moment of casting, however, it is henceforth controlled by the DM, and its personality is prone to change, like any creature.

entries.name: Speech
entries: The object can speak normally, as if it had a mouth, or only to creatures in contact with it.

entries.name: Movement
entries: If the object has moving parts, you can choose to allow the sentience to move those parts, with a Strength and Dexterity of 10. Otherwise it cannot move.

spell.name: Instant Barricade
entries: You create a barricade, no larger than a 10-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}, in an unoccupied space within range, which remains fixed in place for the duration or until it is destroyed. You decide the appearance of the barricade, including its size, shape, and whether it is opaque or transparent. Regardless of your choices, it uses the Instant Barricade stat block.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot:
entries: Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the stat block. The size of the {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} also increases by 5 feet for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Magnet Trap

components.m: an iron rod wrapped in copper wire

entries: You create a powerful magnet which appears at a point you can see within range, which attracts metal in a 10-foot radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} for the duration. Tiny metal objects that aren't being worn or carried in the area fly toward its center.

When a creature enters the area of attraction for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attacks made with metal weapons until the start of its next turn.

Also on a failure, a creature that is made of metal or is wearing metal armor cannot move away from the center of the area until the start of its next turn. On a success, it still must use twice as much movement to move away while within the area of attraction.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot:
entries: The radius of the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} increases by 5 feet for each slot level above 2.

spell.name: Protective Gizmo

components.m: a handful of gears and springs

entries: You construct a magic-imbued machine, custom built for one willing creature that you touch. Choose one ability when you cast the spell. The first time that the target fails a saving throw using the chosen ability before the spell ends, the gizmo fires up, protecting it from the effect. The target then rerolls the saving throw and must use the new roll. The spell then ends with the gizmo falling apart.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot:
entries: The gizmo can fire up without ending the spell once for each slot level above 1.

spell.name: Railgun
entries: You fire the material component from a Ranged weapon you are holding that can fire ammunition of that type. The projectile is launched in a 100-foot {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} that is 5 feet wide, piercing through creatures and objects in its path, including {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}, but is blocked by panels of force, such as the {@spell Wall of Force|XPHB} spell. Creatures and objects in the area take {@damage 4d12} Piercing damage. An unwilling creature can make a Dexterity saving throw to avoid the damage, taking only half as much on a success. If the fired ammunition is a magic item, creatures that fail their save against this spell are affected by the ammunition as if they were hit by it.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot:
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d12} for each slot level above 3. With a level 5+ slot, the ammunition is able to pierce through panels of force.

spell.name: Regenerating Ward
entries: You create a regenerating abjurative force field around a single willing creature you can see within range. For the duration, the target gains a +1 bonus to its AC, and at the start of each of its turns, it gains a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to 2 plus your spellcasting ability modifier. These {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} disappear when the spell ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot:
entries: You can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1.

spell.name: Sever Attunement
entries: You attempt to sever the magical connection between a creature in range that you can see and a magic item of your choice. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the target's {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to the magic item instantly ends, and it loses any benefits that the {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} granted them, though this process fails to remove curses.
If the creature is not attuned to the item or if the item is cursed in a way that prevents {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} ending, the spell fails and nothing happens.

spell.name: Suppress Enchantment
entries: You attempt to suppress the magic of one magic item you can see within range that isn't an Artifact. A suppressed magic item becomes a mundane version of itself for the duration.

A Common or Uncommon item is suppressed automatically. For Rare and rarer magic items, you must make an ability check with your spellcasting modifier against a DC dependent on its rarity, shown on the table below.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot:
entries: You automatically suppress magic items of greater rarity. Rare at level 3, Very Rare at level 4, and Legendary at level 5+.

spell.name: Switch Attunement
entries: You manipulate the magical connection between a willing creature that you touch during the casting time and one magic item that it is attuned to, transferring the connection to a different magic item that no creature is currently attuned to. The target's {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to the original magic item instantly ends, and it loses any benefits granted to it by the {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} (though this process fails to remove curses) and the creature instantly becomes attuned to the new magic item. If the original item is cursed in a way that prevents {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} ending, the spell fails.
Alternatively, you can target two willing creatures with this spell, and have them swap their {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to one magic item each, following the same process.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot:
entries: The casting time changes to: {@variantrule Action|XPHB} with a level 4 slot, or {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} with a level 5+ slot.

spell.name: System Surge

components.m: some zinc and a carbon rod

entries: You supercharge your connection to your magic items, granting yourself the following benefits for the duration:

items: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action granted to you by a magic item as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
When you expend one or more charges on a magic item, roll {@dice 1d4}. On a 4, the charges are not expended.
When you use a magic item that forces creatures to make a saving throw, the DC for the effect increases to match your spell save DC, unless it is already higher.

spell.name: Ward Object
entries: You touch a Tiny or Small object, and magically protect it from harm. For the duration, the object has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to all damage, and cannot be defaced in any way. The object also cannot be forcibly removed from your possession.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot:
entries: You can target larger objects: Medium with a level 3+ slot, Large with a level 4+ slot and Huge with a level 5+ slot.

subclassFeature.name: Forceforger
entries: {@i Master Forcefields as a Tool for Protection}

Forceforgers specialize in creating unique devices that produce force fields - physical manifestations of force which occupy small regions of space. As adventurers, Forceforgers aim to use their force fields to protect themselves and their allies, but also delight in how useful free-standing panels of force can be as a tool for general problem solving.

subclassFeature.name: Forceforger Spells
entries: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Forceforger Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.name: Tool Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency with one set of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice.

entries.name: Armor Crafting
entries: When you craft nonmagical or magic armor, the amount of time required to craft it is halved.

subclassFeature.name: Field Projector
entries: Over the course of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can use {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} to imbue a Tiny non-magical object you touch, such as a gauntlet, with the capabilities of projecting force fields, thereby turning it into your Field Projector. The Field Projector loses its magic if you create another one, but otherwise lasts indefinitely, or until you die.

You can use the Field Projector as a spellcasting focus for your Artificer spells. While you are holding or wearing it, you can cast one of your Forceforger Spells that you have prepared without expending a spell slot, and then cannot do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Force Panel
entries: While you are holding or wearing your Field Projector, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to create a 1 inch thick, 5 foot square transparent panel of force in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of you in an orientation of your choice. The panel has the following properties.

entries.name: Duration
entries: The panel floats in place for one minute, until it is destroyed, or until you create another one.

entries.name: Load Bearing
entries: The panel can support a number of pounds equal to 50 times your Artificer level. If more weight is placed on it, the panel is instantly destroyed.

entries.name: Cover
entries: The panel is an object with an AC of 12 plus your Intelligence modifier and {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Artificer level plus your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}). The panel is solid, blocking movement through it, and it can be used as {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} by Medium and smaller creatures.

entries.name: Damage Redirection
entries: If you or an ally within 5 feet of the panel takes damage from an attack that is of a type other than Poison or Psychic, you can cause an amount of that damage equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1) to be taken by the panel instead of the target (no action required). A panel can take this damage no more than once on a turn.

subclassFeature.name: Forceful Shove
entries: Once per turn when you cast an Artificer spell, you can use your Field Projector to conjure a force field and slam it into one of the spell's targets of your choice. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or take {@damage 1d6} Force damage and be pushed 10 feet in a horizontal direction of your choice.
The force damage increases to {@dice 2d6} when you reach level 15 in this class.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Field Projecter
entries: Your Force Panel feature improves in the following ways:

entries.name: Two Panels
entries: You can now maintain two Force Panels at once. If you create another panel while two exist, you choose which panel is destroyed.

entries.name: Reactive Panel
entries: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is targeted by an attack or makes a Dexterity saving throw, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to create one Force Panel between the target and the source of damage, thereby granting it {@variantrule Cover|XPHB} from the effect.

subclassFeature.name: Flyrokinetic Master
entries: Whenever you would create a Force Panel (whether as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} or as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}), you can instead cast one of your Forceforger spells, provided that spell has a casting time of an action. The spell expends a spell slot as normal. Once you cast a spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Additionally, your Force Panels grant an additional +1 bonus to AC and Dexterity saving throws to creatures benefiting from their {@variantrule Cover|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Magneticist
entries: {@i Manipulate Metal from Afar}

Magneticists develop special devices which grant them power over magnetism, allowing them to move and manipulate metal objects to their will. These artificers are famed for their extraordinary skill with thrown weapons, using their mastery of magnetism to recall their weapons and strike with deadly force.

subclassFeature.name: Magneticist Spells
entries: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Magneticist Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Magneticist Spells
rows: {@spell Jump|XPHB}, {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB}, {@spell Tenser's Floating Disk|XPHB}
{@spell Conjure Volley|XPHB}, {@spell Wall of Stone|XPHB} (as metal)

entries.name: Tool Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency with one set of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice.

entries.name: Weapon Crafting
entries: When you craft a nonmagical or magic weapon, the amount of time required to craft it is halved.

subclassFeature.name: Magnetic Manipulator
entries: Over the course of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can use {@item Smith's tools|XPHB} to imbue a non-magical piece of clothing or armor you touch, such as a bracer, with the capabilities of controlling magnetic fields, thereby turning it into your Magnetic Manipulator. The Magnetic Manipulator loses its magic if you create another one. It otherwise lasts indefinitely or until you die. You can use the Magnetic Manipulator as a spellcasting focus for your Artificer spells.

entries.name: Move Metal
entries: While wearing the manipulator, when you start casting {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB}, you can modify it for that casting. If you do, the hand is {@condition invisible}, it can only manipulate metal objects, and the weight it can carry is tripled.

entries.name: Metal Objects
entries: For the purposes of using your subclass features, a metal object is any object that is by weight at least 50% made of metal material, or contains metal reinforcement. For example, a wooden chest with iron bands would count as a metal object, but a wooden chest with an iron lock would not.

subclassFeature.name: Metal Slinger
entries: You have trained extensively with thrown weapons, using your manipulation of magnetic fields to propel the weapons, rather than relying on your physicality. You gain proficiency with all weapons with the {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} property, and while wearing your Magnetic Manipulator, you gain the following benefits when making ranged weapon attacks with thrown weapons that are made of metal:

entries.name: Intelligent Attacks
entries: You can use your Intelligence modifier, instead of Strength or Dexterity, for the attack and damage rolls.

entries.name: Magnetic Recall
entries: Immediately after hitting or missing an attack, you can recall the weapon to your hand.

entries.name: Improved Range
entries: The weapon's normal range increases to 60 feet, if it was not already higher.

entries.name: Magnetic Pull
entries: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the same type as the weapon and if the target is Medium or smaller, you can move it 10 feet directly toward or away from you. You can only do so once per turn.
When you reach Artificer level 9, the extra damage increases to {@dice 2d6} and you can move Large creatures.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Metallic Control
entries: You have upgraded the capabilities of your Magnetic Manipulator, drastically increasing your control over metal. While wearing your Magnetic Manipulator, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to cast the {@spell Telekinesis|XPHB} spell without a spell slot. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for this spell, and the spell is altered in the following ways:

entries.name: Metal Only
entries: You can only target metal objects, metal creatures, and creatures wearing metal armor.

entries.name: Deft Control
entries: You take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to attempt to maintain your telekinetic grip, instead of a {@action Magic|XPHB} action.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Alternatively, you can use it by expending a level 5+ spell slot, if you have any.

subclassFeature.name: Metallic Cloud
entries: Your control over metal has reached its pinnacle, allowing you near-thoughtless, yet extremely fine, control over many small pieces of metal. Provided you have access to at least 5 lbs of Tiny pieces of loose metal and are wearing your Magnetic Manipulator, you gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Protective Shell
entries: {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB|Critical Hits} made against you count as normal hits while you don't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, as the metal forms a barrier around your vital areas.

entries.name: Non-Metallic Control
entries: You can now target non-metallic creatures and objects with your Metallic Control and Move Metal features by surrounding the target with the metal fragments.

entries.name: Improved Pull
entries: You can now use Magnetic Pull on each attack you make, not just once per turn.

subclassFeature.name: Mutagenist
entries: {@i Mutate your Body into a Deadly Weapon}

Mutagenists are fuelled—obsessed even—by the notion that the body is just another object to be enhanced with magic. These mad scientists infuse their bodies with experimental substances called mutagens to become faster or stronger, or to grant themselves potent magical abilities. Some Artificers on this path lose themselves to their obsession, becoming monstrously altered, while others strike a balance between flesh and formula.

subclassFeature.name: Mutagenist Spells
entries: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Mutagenist Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.name: Tool Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice.

entries.name: Potion Crafting
entries: When you brew a potion using the crafting rules in the Dungeon Master's Guide, the amount of time required to craft it is halved.

subclassFeature.name: Mutagenic Concoctions
entries: Your experimentation on your own body has permanently altered your physiology and has made you particularly accepting of your self-enhancing concoctions. You gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Concoction Casting
entries: When you start casting one of your Mutagenist spells and target only yourself, you can alter the casting process by quaffing, injecting, or otherwise consuming a special concoction that you quickly brew. When you cast a spell in this way, it has no Verbal or Somatic components, but you must use {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} as a spellcasting focus. You do not need to concentrate on the spell. You can only be affected by one spell in this way at a time, and the previous spell ends immediately if you use Concoction Casting again.

entries.name: Free Casting
entries: You can cast one of your Mutagenist spells that you have prepared without expending a spell slot, provided that you do so with Concoction Casting. You then can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Intelligent Attacks
entries: While under the effects of one or more of your Mutagenist spells, you can make weapon attacks using your Intelligence modifier, instead of Strength or Dexterity.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Body Modifications
entries: When you use your Replicate Magic Item feature to create a magic item, you can augment your body parts with the power of the chosen magic item, instead of manifesting the item like normal. The item must be a Melee weapon, a suit of armor, or a Wondrous Item that is otherwise worn.
While the magic item is active, you gain a special benefit, depending on what kind of item it is.

entries.name: Armor
entries: While you're not wearing armor, your AC is equal to 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Intelligence modifier and your skin gains the magical properties of the magic item. You can use a {@item Shield|XPHB} and still gain these benefits. Furthermore, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by an amount equal to your Artificer level. Your body can only be augmented in this way by one magic item at a time.

entries.name: Weapon
entries: You form a facsimile of the magic item on your body, which has the magic item's magical properties. You're proficient with the weapon, you can't be disarmed of it, and you don't need to use your hands to wield it. Furthermore, when you hit with this weapon, it deals an extra {@damage 1d4} damage of the weapon's damage type.

entries.name: Worn Item
entries: The magic item is infused into the region of the body it would normally be worn. For example, you would infuse your hands in place of creating a pair of gloves. A given body part can only be augmented by one of these magic items, but you are not prevented from benefiting from a magic item worn over it. You can create one such magic item without it counting against the number of magic items you can have at once.

subclassFeature.name: Master Mutagenist
entries: Your body has adapted to become even more accepting of your Mutagenic Concoctions. You can now benefit from two Concoction Castings at the same time. If you cast a third spell with Concoction Casting, one of the two active spells ends (you choose which).

subclassFeature.name: Portalweaver
entries: {@i Master the Magic of Translocation}

Portalweavers are fascinated by manipulations of space, and how magic can be used to link two different spatial points with phenomena known as portals. These artificers develop devices for their own use to form these portals, allowing them to rival the translocation capabilities of even the most powerful conjurers.

subclassFeature.name: Portalweaver Spells
entries: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Portalweaver Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.name: Tool Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with {@item Weaver's Tools|XPHB}. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency with one set of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice.

entries.name: Garment Crafting
entries: When you craft nonmagical or magic clothing, the amount of time required to craft it is halved.

subclassFeature.name: Portal Device
entries: Over the course of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can use {@item Weaver's tools|XPHB} to imbue a Tiny non-magical object you touch, such as a {@item Light Crossbow|XPHB}, with the capability of creating portals, thereby turning it into your Portal Device. The Portal Device loses its magic if you create another one, but otherwise lasts indefinitely or until you die. You must be holding or wearing the Portal Device to use it.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use the Portal Device as a spellcasting focus for your Artificer spells.

entries.name: Free Casting
entries: While you are holding or wearing your Portal Device, you can cast one of your Portalweaver spells you have prepared without expending a spell slot. You then can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Portal Range
entries: Some features in this subclass refer to your Portal Range, which is 20 feet. Your Portal Range increases to 40 feet when you reach level 9 in this class, and to 60 feet when you reach level 15 in this class.

subclassFeature.name: Portals
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can use your Portal Device to create linked portals in two Medium-sized unoccupied spaces you can see within your Portal Range that aren't behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}. The portals appear as 5-foot diameter circular rings of colored light which hover in place, perpendicular to the ground, facing a direction of your choice. The portals have the following properties.

entries.name: Duration
entries: The portals last for 1 minute, or until you stop {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on them, as if you were {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on a spell.

entries.name: Relocate Portal
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can move one portal to an unoccupied space within your Portal Range. The new location can't be more than twice your Portal Range away from the other portal.

entries.name: Moving through Portals
entries: The portals are open on one side only, and can be passed through by anything that fits through them, including attacks and area effects, provided they are no wider than 5 feet at the point that they pass through. Anything that enters the open side of a portal exits from the open side of the other portal as if the two were adjacent to each other.

entries.name: Closing Portals
entries: If a creature is part-way through the portals when they close or move, it is instantly shunted to an unoccupied space on one side of its choice.

entries.name: Visibility
entries: The open side of each portal can be seen through, the other appearing opaque. When looking through a portal, a creature sees through the open face of the connected portal.

entries.name: Uses
entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Mini Portals
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can use your Portal Device to create two Tiny portals: one in your space and the other in an unoccupied space you can see within your Portal Range that isn't behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}. The portals disappear immediately after you use them, or at the end of your turn if unused. You can use these portals in the following ways.

entries.name: Interact
entries: You can interact with an object as if you were in the far portal's space, and can move Tiny objects through the portals.

entries.name: Attack
entries: You can direct an attack you make or an Artificer spell you cast through the portals, making it as if you were in the far portal's space. The spell must have a duration of 'Instantaneous' or a range of 'Touch'. You can add {@damage 1d8} to one damage roll of the attack or spell, if it deals damage.

subclassFeature.name: Persistent Portals
entries: Your Portals feature no longer requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}. You can close the portals early at any time (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Portal Master
entries: You have infused your Portal Device with the ability to transport people to destinations near and far. You can use it to cast one of the following spells without expending a spell slot, and then can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}: {@spell Arcane Gate|XPHB}, {@spell Plane Shift|XPHB}, {@spell Scatter|XGE}, or {@spell Teleport|XPHB}. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.
